name: DriftQCap Official Data
description: DriftQCap-v1.0 is a benchmark dataset for few-shot quantum capability prediction under distribution shift. The release contains three CSV splits: a controlled synthetic benchmark, a telemetry-anchored semi-synthetic IBM split seeded from backend calibration snapshots, and a thin executed-hardware pilot combined with the frozen synthetic source split. It supports evaluation of source-to-target adaptation, label acquisition, and calibration/interval metrics under drift.

distribution.name: driftqcap_synthetic_paper_rf_v3.csv
distribution.description: Controlled synthetic DriftQCap benchmark split containing source and target episodes across structured shift families. Circuit structures, drift mechanisms, and labels are generated by the DriftQCap pipeline.

distribution.name: real_eval_with_synthetic_source.csv
distribution.description: Thin executed-hardware pilot evaluation file. It combines the frozen synthetic source split with target rows derived from executed IBM hardware pilot runs. This split is intended as a limited sanity check, not as the headline benchmark result.

distribution.name: semi_synth_from_snapshots_clean.csv
distribution.description: Telemetry-anchored semi-synthetic split using real IBM backend calibration/noise telemetry from Fez, Kingston, and Marrakesh. Circuit structures and labels are generated using the DriftQCap pipeline conditioned on these telemetry snapshots.

rai:dataCollection: The release was assembled from three sources. First, a controlled synthetic benchmark was generated by the DriftQCap pipeline to instantiate source and target episodes under noise, depth, circuit-family, temporal, and compound shifts. Second, a telemetry-anchored semi-synthetic split was generated by conditioning the same benchmark pipeline on real IBM backend calibration snapshots, including coherence, readout, and gate-error telemetry from Fez, Kingston, and Marrakesh. Third, a thin executed-hardware pilot split was built by combining the frozen synthetic source split with target rows derived from actual IBM hardware pilot runs.
rai:dataCollectionRawData: Raw inputs include generated circuit descriptors, generated synthetic drift episodes, IBM backend calibration/noise telemetry snapshots (T1, T2, readout error, one-qubit error-per-gate, two-qubit error-per-gate, and topology-derived summaries), and a limited set of executed-hardware pilot rows. The public release contains processed CSV files rather than API credentials, account metadata, or private job metadata.
rai:dataCollectionMissingData: No human-subject attributes are collected. Some hardware-pilot rows may have unavailable backend or execution metadata after anonymization or schema alignment. The benchmark pipeline is designed to operate on the released schema; missing optional metadata should not be interpreted as device absence or failed execution.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: All files were converted to DriftQCap-compatible CSV schemas with shared columns for episode identifiers, source/target domain, shift type, circuit descriptors, noise descriptors, and target labels.
The executed-hardware pilot was combined with the frozen synthetic source split so that the same source-to-target adaptation protocol can be run on real target rows.
Account identifiers, API tokens, local file paths, and personal metadata were removed before release.

Columns beginning with _hidden_ and columns ending in _component are generator diagnostics or decomposition terms included for auditability; they are not intended as model-visible features in the benchmark protocol.
rai:dataManipulationProtocol: Synthetic and telemetry-anchored labels were generated by the DriftQCap pipeline using circuit descriptors and noise/telemetry parameters. The real-hardware pilot file was schema-aligned and combined with the frozen synthetic source split for evaluation. Evaluation code excludes generator-diagnostic columns from the model-visible feature set; users should follow the released scripts and paper protocol to avoid leakage.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: The dataset does not use human annotation. Labels are produced by the benchmark generator for the synthetic and telemetry-anchored semi-synthetic splits. The executed-hardware pilot uses derived circuit-level outcomes from hardware runs after schema normalization.
rai:dataAnnotationPlatform: Not applicable: no crowdsourcing or human-labeling platform was used.
rai:dataAnnotationAnalysis: Because labels are generated or derived rather than manually annotated, inter-annotator agreement is not applicable.
For the benchmark protocol, statistical comparisons should be performed at the episode level rather than the circuit-row level to avoid pseudoreplication.
rai:annotationsPerItem: Not applicable for human annotation. Each row contains one error_rate target and, where present, a derived pass_label.
rai:annotatorDemographics: Not applicable: no human annotators were used.
rai:machineAnnotationTools: IBM backend telemetry access through the project collection pipeline.
Python data-processing stack for CSV/schema alignment.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: Version 1.0 is an archival release corresponding to the submitted paper artifact.
Future versions may add cleaned public-feature-only CSVs, expanded hardware pilots, code snapshots, or additional backend telemetry, while preserving this DOI/version record.
Known issues or clarifications will be documented through subsequent Dataverse versions rather than by silently replacing the released files.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: The dataset contains no human-subject data and no demographic, biometric, health, financial, or social attributes.
The release should not contain API tokens, account credentials, personal emails, or local filesystem paths. Backend names and hardware timestamps are included only as quantum-device metadata.
The anonymous creator metadata is used only to preserve double-blind review where applicable.
rai:dataSocialImpact: The dataset is intended to improve evaluation rigor for quantum capability prediction by making drift, label-budget sensitivity, acquisition policy, and calibration failure modes explicit. Potential positive impact includes more transparent reporting of when predictors fail under deployment-like shift. Potential negative impact would arise if users overinterpret the benchmark as a vendor ranking, as a replacement for large-scale real-hardware validation, or as evidence of guaranteed device performance. The paper and metadata explicitly discourage those uses.
rai:dataBiases: The controlled synthetic benchmark reflects the selected circuit families, depth ranges, topology summaries, and drift mechanisms implemented in DriftQCap; it does not represent all quantum algorithms or all device architectures.

The telemetry-anchored semi-synthetic split is conditioned on IBM backend snapshots from Fez, Kingston, and Marrakesh and may not generalize to other vendors, processor generations, calibration policies, or noise regimes.
The executed-hardware pilot is intentionally small and should be interpreted only as a limited sanity check, not as a statistically comprehensive real-hardware validation set.

Synthetic and semi-synthetic labels can encode assumptions from the generator; users should report results separately for synthetic, telemetry-anchored semi-synthetic, and executed-hardware pilot regimes.
rai:dataLimitations: The primary benchmark is synthetic and is designed for controlled stress-testing of few-shot adaptation under drift, not for universal claims about real hardware.

The telemetry-anchored split uses real IBM calibration/noise parameters but generated circuit structures and generated labels; it should be described as telemetry-anchored semi-synthetic, not as full real-hardware validation.
The executed-hardware pilot is thin and may be noisy or underpowered for headline performance claims.
The dataset should not be used for procurement decisions, vendor ranking, safety certification, or claims of guaranteed performance on unseen quantum devices.
Generator-diagnostic columns are included for auditability but should not be used as model-visible features unless explicitly studying leakage or simulator diagnostics.
rai:dataUseCases: Benchmarking quantum capability predictors under structured source-to-target distribution shift.
Evaluating few-shot target adaptation under explicit label budgets.
Comparing deployable acquisition policies such as random, diversity-based, and uncertainty-based selection.
Evaluating calibration, conformal interval coverage, and reliability under drift.
Reproducing the DriftQCap paper's data-side analyses using the released CSV splits.
Developing future source predictors, including qpa-style or graph-based models, within a fixed drift-evaluation protocol.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: DriftQCap generator source code, fixed splits, and random seed/configuration records
prov:wasDerivedFrom.description: Synthetic and telemetry-anchored rows are derived from the DriftQCap generator and its released configuration, fixed split definitions, random seeds, and evaluation scripts.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: IBM Quantum backend calibration/noise telemetry snapshots
prov:wasDerivedFrom.description: Device-state covariates for the telemetry-anchored split are seeded from IBM backend telemetry for Fez, Kingston, and Marrakesh, including T1, T2, readout error, one-qubit EPG, two-qubit EPG, topology summaries, and timestamps. No API credentials or private account metadata are included.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: Author-collected thin IBM executed-hardware pilot runs
prov:wasDerivedFrom.description: The thin executed-hardware pilot rows in real_eval_with_synthetic_source.csv are derived from author-run IBM hardware executions and schema-aligned with the frozen synthetic source split. This source is intended only as a limited sanity check, not as the primary benchmark evidence.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Controlled synthetic benchmark generation
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: The DriftQCap pipeline generated source and target episodes under noise, depth, circuit-family, temporal, and compound shifts. Circuit descriptors, device-state covariates, error_rate labels, pass_label values, and generator diagnostic columns were produced automatically by deterministic scripts using released fixed splits and random seeds. No LLM, crowdworker, or human annotation was used.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Telemetry-anchored semi-synthetic generation
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: IBM backend calibration/noise telemetry snapshots from Fez, Kingston, and Marrakesh were collected as device-state seeds. Synthetic circuit structures and labels were then generated by the DriftQCap pipeline conditioned on those telemetry values. The resulting rows should be interpreted as telemetry-anchored semi-synthetic data, not as full executed-hardware validation.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Executed-hardware pilot collection and preprocessing
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: A thin pilot set of circuits was executed on IBM hardware, and measured outcomes were converted into schema-compatible rows with error_rate/pass_label fields. The pilot was combined with the frozen synthetic source split for limited sanity-check evaluation. Local paths, credentials, API tokens, private emails, and account metadata were excluded from the public release.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Schema normalization, leakage audit, and quality review
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Released CSVs were normalized to a common schema, checked for source-target episode and circuit-ID disjointness, and documented with limitations and intended-use constraints. Generator-diagnostic columns are retained for auditability but should not be used as model-visible features unless explicitly studying leakage or simulator diagnostics.
